    ↑↓ navigate ↵ open esc close

    Stop burning tokens on noise

    Every token counts. Make yours go further.

    API limits are tighter than ever. lean-ctx compresses file reads, CLI output, and context windows — so you get more done before hitting your quota. Single Rust binary. Zero cloud.

    up to 99% Token Reduction
    90+ CLI Patterns
    24 Intelligent Tools
    Graph Project Index
    CCP CEP TDD Built-in cost optimization protocols
    Get Started Star on GitHub
    lean-ctx gain real session data

    $ lean-ctx gain

    ◆ lean-ctx Token Savings Dashboard

    ────────────────────────────────────────────

    1.7Msaved 76.8%rate 520cmds $4.25USD

    Since 2026-03-23 (2 days) ▁█

    $ lean-ctx gain

    ◆ lean-ctx Token Savings

    1.7M saved 76.8% 520 cmds $4.25

    Top Commands

    curl 48x ████████████ 728K

    git 34x ██████ 375K

    ctx_read 103x █ 59K

    You're paying for tokens your LLM doesn't need.

    ~65%

    of file reads are re-reads

    Wasted re-reads

    In multi-step coding sessions, the same files are read over and over. Every redundant read burns tokens from your quota — tokens that could go toward actual reasoning.

    $20–200

    per month on AI tools

    Hard limits, real money

    500 requests/day. 45 messages per 5 hours. 1,500 premium requests/month. Every provider caps your usage — and most of your tokens go to boilerplate.

    200K

    token context windows

    Bigger windows, same waste

    Larger context windows don't help when they're filled with noise. 10K focused tokens outperform 200K of boilerplate — and cost a fraction.

    Before & After

    Same information. Fraction of the tokens.

    Real output from real coding sessions — click to compare.

    Without LeanCTX ~3,517 tokens 
    $ Read auth.ts
import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
import { db } from './db.js';

const SESSION_EXPIRY = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;

export function generateToken(): string {
  return createHash('sha256')
    .update(Math.random().toString())
    .digest('hex');
}

export async function createUser(
  email: string, password: string,
  name: string, locale: string
) {
  const hash = await hashPassword(password);
  const token = generateToken();
  // ... 123 lines total
    With LeanCTX ~13 tokens (-99%) 
    $ ctx_read auth.ts
F1 [cached 2t 123L ∅]
    Without LeanCTX ~2,536 tokens 
    $ Read db.ts
import { Pool } from 'pg';
import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/node-postgres';
import * as schema from './schema.js';

const pool = new Pool({
  connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  max: 20,
  idleTimeoutMillis: 30000,
});

export const db = drizzle(pool, { schema });

export async function query<T>(
  sql: string, params: unknown[]
): Promise<T[]> {
  const result = await pool.query(sql, params);
  return result.rows as T[];
}
// ... 89 lines of implementation
    With LeanCTX ~252 tokens (-90%) 
    $ ctx_read db.ts --mode signatures
F2 [89L +]
fn ⊛ query<T>(sql:s, params:unknown[]) → T[]
fn ⊛ migrate()
fn ⊛ transaction<T>(fn:(tx) → T) → T
fn ⊛ healthCheck() → boolean
cl Database
  ⊛ connect()
  ⊛ disconnect()
[2284 tok saved (90%)]
    Without LeanCTX ~847 tokens 
    $ npm install express

added 65 packages, and audited 66 packages in 2s

12 packages are looking for funding
  run `npm fund` for details

found 0 vulnerabilities

npm warn deprecated inflight@1.0.6: This module is
not supported, and leaks memory. Do not use it. Check
out lru-cache if you want a good and tested way to
coalesce async requests by a key value, which is much
more comprehensive and powerful.

npm warn deprecated glob@7.2.3: Glob versions prior
to v9 are no longer supported
    With LeanCTX ~24 tokens (-97%) 
    $ ctx_shell "npm install express"
+express@4.18.2 (65 deps, 2s)
[823 tok saved (97%)]
    Without LeanCTX ~612 tokens 
    $ git status
On branch feature/auth
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/feature/auth' by 3 commits.
  (use "git push" to publish your local commits)

Changes to be committed:
  (use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage)
	new file:   src/auth/session.ts
	modified:   src/auth/login.ts
	modified:   src/routes/api.ts

Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
  (use "git restore <file>..." to discard changes in working directory)
	modified:   src/auth/session.ts

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	src/auth/session.test.ts
    With LeanCTX ~78 tokens (-87%) 
    $ ctx_shell "git status"
feature/auth ↑3
staged: +src/auth/session.ts ~src/auth/login.ts ~src/routes/api.ts
unstaged: ~src/auth/session.ts
untracked: src/auth/session.test.ts
[534 tok saved (87%)]
    Without LeanCTX ~1,240 tokens 
    $ tsc --noEmit
src/auth/session.ts(12,5): error TS2322: Type 'string | undefined'
is not assignable to type 'string'.
  Type 'undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'.

src/auth/session.ts(24,9): error TS2345: Argument of type 'number'
is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.

src/routes/api.ts(8,3): error TS2739: Type '{ id: number; }'
is missing the following properties from type 'User':
name, email, createdAt

src/routes/api.ts(45,28): error TS2551: Property 'stauts' does
not exist on type 'Response'. Did you mean 'status'?

Found 4 errors in 2 files.

Errors  Files
     2  src/auth/session.ts:12
     2  src/routes/api.ts:8
    With LeanCTX ~142 tokens (-89%) 
    $ ctx_shell "tsc --noEmit"
4 errors in 2 files:
session.ts:12 TS2322 string|undefined ≠ string
session.ts:24 TS2345 number ≠ string
api.ts:8 TS2739 {id} missing name,email,createdAt
api.ts:45 TS2551 'stauts' → 'status'
[1098 tok saved (89%)]

  core/ (9)
  tools/ (7)

  patterns/ (5)
  cli.ts
  index.ts
[392 tok saved (40%)]
    Without LeanCTX ~28,450 tokens 
    $ Read all 12 cached files again...

// auth.ts — 123 lines
import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
...

// db.ts — 89 lines
import { Pool } from 'pg';
...

// routes.ts — 234 lines
import { Router } from 'express';
...

// ... 9 more files, full content
// Total: 28,450 tokens re-sent
    With LeanCTX ~890 tokens (-97%) 
    $ ctx_compress
CTX CHECKPOINT (12 files)

F1 auth.ts [123L]: fn generateToken,
  fn createUser, fn verifyEmail, ...
F2 db.ts [89L]: cl Database, fn query,
  fn migrate
F3 routes.ts [234L]: fn setupRoutes,
  fn authMiddleware

STATS: 34 reads, 22 hits (65%)
COMPRESSION: 28450 → 890 tok (97%)
    Without LeanCTX ~1,856 tokens 
    $ Read middleware.ts
import express from 'express';
import type { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';

/**
 * @description Authentication middleware
 * @param {Request} req - Express request
 * @param {Response} res - Express response
 * @param {NextFunction} next - Next function
 * @returns {void}
 */
export function authMiddleware(
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
  next: NextFunction
): void {
  const token = req.headers.authorization;
  if (!token) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
    return;
  }
  // ... 78 lines, mostly JSDoc + boilerplate
    With LeanCTX ~312 tokens (-83%) 
    $ ctx_read middleware.ts --mode entropy
F8 [78L +] (H̄=3.2 bits/char)
⊘ 14 low-entropy lines (H<2.0)
⊘ 3 duplicate patterns (J≥0.7)
fn authMiddleware(req,res,next)
  token = req.headers.authorization
  if !token → 401
  verify(token) → req.user
fn rateLimiter(max,window)
  ...
[1544 tok saved (83%)]

    Terminal Dashboard — Real Session Data

    See your savings
    at a glance.

    ANSI colors, Unicode bars, sparklines, and USD cost estimates. Track every token across all sessions.

    Lifetime statistics — persisted in ~/.lean-ctx/stats.json

    Three views — gain, --graph, --daily

    USD cost tracking — real savings per session

    $ lean-ctx gain

    $ lean-ctx gain

    Trend: ▅█▆█▁█

    Your Token Budget, Optimized

    24 tools that cut your token spend.

    Every tool reduces what gets sent to the LLM. Fewer tokens billed, more messages in your quota, lower API costs.

    Explore all tools

    ctx_read

    up to 99%

    6 compression modes + session caching. Re-reads cost 13 tokens.

    ctx_graph

    Structural

    Persistent project graph with incremental scanning. 5 actions: build, related, symbol, impact, status.

    ctx_shell

    60–90%

    90+ patterns across 34 categories — git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, aws, prisma, helm, and more.

    ctx_intent

    Context-aware

    Graph-enhanced intent detection. Classifies queries and auto-loads relevant files with optimal compression.

    ctx_search

    50–80%

    Pattern search returning only matching lines with compact context.

    ctx_compress

    90–95%

    Checkpoint all cached files into an ultra-compact signature map.

    ctx_smart_read

    Automatic

    Bayesian mode prediction — automatically picks the optimal compression per file type and size.

    ctx_benchmark

    Data-driven

    Real project benchmarks with token measurements, latency, and AST preservation scores.

    ctx_metrics

    Real-time

    Real-time session stats: cache hits, compression ratios, costs.

    ctx_session

    CCP

    Cross-session memory — persist task, findings, decisions across chats and context compactions.

    ctx_wrapped

    Report

    Shareable savings report — "Spotify Wrapped" for your token savings with cost tracking.

    CEP Engine

    Smart

    Cognitive Efficiency Protocol — adaptive complexity, quality scoring, auto-validation, and CEP compliance metrics.

    lctx

    One command

    Multi-agent launcher. Auto-detects Cursor, Claude, Codex, Gemini, Windsurf, Cline and configures lean-ctx.

    lean-ctx init

    Auto-setup

    23 shell aliases installed globally with --global. macOS, Linux, Windows.

    lean-ctx gain

    Persistent

    Visual terminal dashboard with lifetime savings and USD cost tracking.

    Explore all tools

    Beyond Compression

    Not just smaller. Smarter.

    lean-ctx v2.7.0 goes beyond token compression. It understands your project structure, launches any AI agent with one command, and installs without friction.

    Persistent Project Graph

    New in v2.7.0

    Your codebase, mapped.

    Incremental scanning builds a persistent dependency graph of your entire project. Find related files, trace symbol usage, and measure impact — without re-scanning every session.

    build Scan & persist project structure
    related BFS traversal for connected files
    symbol Jump to any exported symbol
    impact Reverse dependency analysis
    status Index metadata & freshness
    Learn more
    $ ctx_graph action=build

    GRAPH BUILD — /Users/you/project

    ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

    Scanned: 142 files (38 new, 104 cached)

    Symbols: 847 exports across 12 languages

    Edges: 1,203 import relationships

     

    Index: ~/.lean-ctx/graphs/a3f8c1/index.json

    Build time: 340ms (incremental)

    Multi-Agent Launcher

    New in v2.7.0

    One command. Any AI agent.

    Auto-detects your AI tool (Cursor, Claude, Gemini, Codex, Windsurf, Cline), builds the project graph, and launches with lean-ctx pre-configured. Zero manual setup.

    lctx Auto-detect agent & launch
    lctx cursor Launch specific agent
    lctx --list Show supported agents
    Learn more
    $ lctx

    ◆ lean-ctx launcher

    ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

    Detected: cursor (running)

    Project: ~/projects/my-app

    Graph: 142 files, 847 symbols ✓

     

    Launching Cursor with lean-ctx...

    Context server: connected (24 tools)

    Universal Installation

    New in v2.7.0

    Five ways to install. Zero friction.

    From a one-line curl command to npm, Homebrew, Cargo, or AUR — install lean-ctx however you prefer. No Rust toolchain required for binary installs.

    curl | sh Universal one-liner with SHA256 verification
    npm i -g lean-ctx-bin Node.js binary distribution
    brew install Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
    cargo install From source via crates.io
    yay -S Arch Linux (AUR)
    Learn more
    $ curl -fsSL https://leanctx.com/install.sh | sh

    ◆ lean-ctx installer

    ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

    OS: darwin-aarch64

    Source: github.com/yvgude/lean-ctx/releases

    SHA256: verified ✓

     

    Installed: /usr/local/bin/lean-ctx

    Version: 2.7.0 — ready.

    Three Protocols, One Goal

    Compress smarter. Spend less.

    Each protocol targets a different layer of token waste — from how files are read to how sessions persist to how the LLM communicates.

    CEP Cognitive Efficiency Protocol v2.7.0

    Measure and optimize how the LLM processes context

    • › Compliance score (0-100) across cache, mode diversity, compression, action-first
    • › Adaptive complexity — Mechanical / Standard / Architectural task classification
    • › Quality scorer ensures >95% AST and identifier preservation
    • › Auto-validation pipeline for Rust, JS/TS, Python, JSON, TOML
    Learn more
    cep 
    CEP SCORE: 87/100
Cache: 92%  Modes: 4/6  Compression: 78%
Task: Standard (feature implementation)
Quality: AST 98% | Identifiers 97% | Lines 96%
    CCP Context Continuity Protocol v2.0.0

    Cross-session memory that eliminates cold starts

    • › Persists task, findings, decisions, and file access across chats
    • › LITM-aware positioning for optimal attention placement
    • › Load previous sessions in ~400 tokens (vs ~50K cold start)
    • › Auto-save every 5 tool calls + idle timeout protection
    Learn more
    ccp 
    ACTIVE SESSION v125 | 513 calls | 176K tok saved
Task: CEP v1 — local verification
Findings: 3 | Decisions: 2 | Files: 17
Load time: 400 tokens (99.2% less than cold start)
    TDD Token Dense Dialect v1.7.0

    Symbol shorthand for compact LLM communication

    • › Unicode symbols: λ=function, §=struct, ∂=trait, τ=type, ε=enum
    • › ROI-based identifier mapping (long names → α1, α2...)
    • › Action vocabulary: ⊕=add, ⊖=remove, ∆=modify, ✓=ok, ✗=fail
    • › Filler detection v2 — removes 60+ hedge/meta/closing patterns
    Learn more
    tdd 
    §MAP: α1=UserAuthService α2=validateToken
λ+α2(⊕, req:HttpReq) → Result<Token>
  ∆ verify_jwt → verify_rs256  // was HS256
  ⊕ rate_limit: 100/min
  ✓ tests: 42 passed

    The Intelligence Layer

    Less tokens. Better results.

    Input Efficiency

    Implemented

    Compression Layer

    AST-based signatures, delta-loading, session caching, entropy filtering. Send the skeleton, not the flesh.

    Structural Awareness

    Implemented

    Project Intelligence

    Persistent project graph with incremental scanning. Dependency mapping, symbol lookup, impact analysis, and graph-based intent detection.

    Memory Architecture

    Implemented

    Context Manager

    Session cache with auto-TTL, context checkpoints, subagent isolation. Cross-session memory via CCP. The model sees the latest state, not the full history.

    Better Output

    Implemented

    Quality Guardrail

    CEP compliance scoring, AST preservation validation, adaptive complexity classification. Less noise = more attention on logic nodes = better code.

    The goal:

    10K tokens that outperform 200K.

    Universal compatibility

    One filter. Every AI tool.

    LeanCTX works as the intelligence layer between you and any AI coding tool. Wherever tokens flow, lean-ctx maximizes their impact.

    ~40

    file reads per session

    ~85K

    tokens of context noise

    ~9K

    with LeanCTX (89% less)

    Based on a typical multi-step coding session with ~40 file reads across a medium-sized project. Savings measured per tool operation using tiktoken (o200k_base).

    Cursor

    Price $20 — $200/mo
    Limits 500 fast, then on-demand
    Context Up to 200K tokens

    Credits stretch ~3x further

    GitHub Copilot

    Price Free — $39/mo
    Limits 50-1,500 premium req/mo
    Context Up to 200K tokens

    Better context per request

    Claude Code

    Price $20 — $200/mo
    Limits ~45 msgs/5h (Pro)
    Context 200K tokens

    Sessions ~3x longer

    Windsurf

    Price $15 — $60/mo
    Limits 500 credits/mo (Pro)
    Context 200K tokens

    Credits last ~2x longer

    Zed AI

    Price $10 — $50/mo
    Limits Credit-based
    Context Up to 128K tokens

    More completions per credit

    Cline

    Price Bring your API key
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 200K tokens

    Direct API cost reduction

    Continue

    Price Free + API costs
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 200K tokens

    Fewer tokens per autocomplete

    Aider

    Price Free + API costs
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 128K tokens

    Lower per-session API bills

    OpenAI Codex

    Price Bring your API key
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 200K tokens

    Smaller context, sharper output

    Gemini CLI

    Price Bring your API key
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 1M tokens

    Higher signal-to-noise ratio

    OpenCode

    Price Free + API costs
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 200K tokens

    Less noise per completion

    OpenClaw

    Price Free + API costs
    Limits Pay per token (API)
    Context 200K tokens

    Shell hook works out of the box

    Get started

    Running in 60 seconds.

    No cloud account. No API keys. No subscription.

    01

    Install

    curl -fsSL https://leanctx.com/install.sh | sh

    or brew install yvgude/lean-ctx/lean-ctx · cargo install lean-ctx · npm i -g lean-ctx-bin

    02

    Setup

    lean-ctx setup — auto-detects shell + editors, configures everything

    03

    Restart shell

    source ~/.zshrc — or restart your terminal

    04

    Watch your savings

    Every read now goes through the intelligence layer. Run ctx_metrics to see stats.

    {
  "mcpServers": {
    "lean-ctx": {
      "command": "lean-ctx"
    }
  }
}
    {
  "servers": {
    "lean-ctx": {
      "command": "lean-ctx"
    }
  }
}
    claude mcp add lean-ctx lean-ctx
    {
  "mcpServers": {
    "lean-ctx": {
      "command": "lean-ctx"
    }
  }
}

    $ ctx_metrics

    LeanCTX session metrics

    Files tracked: 12 | Reads: 34 | Cache hits: 22 (65%)

    Input tokens: 28.5K → 4.1K sent | 24.3K saved (85%)

    Cost estimate: $0.071 → $0.010 | $0.061 saved

    Open Source, Free, Local

    Stop wasting tokens. Start saving.

    Make every token count. Extend your limits. Cut your costs. One binary, zero cloud dependencies.

    Single Rust binary MIT licensed No cloud
    Get Started Free View on GitHub

    The Intelligence Layer for AI Coding.
